title: Sound: Buzzer
subtitle: hear, feel, explore!
overview: Students explore how an active buzzer creates sound using electricity. They hear the buzzing sound, observe vibrations and understand how buzzers are used in real-life devices like alarms, toys and school bells.

images._note: Add photos from the actual classroom activity with buzzers and batteries.

keyTopics: Battery gives power to the buzzer
Buzzers are used in alarms and bells

youtubeVideos.title: What is a Buzzer?
youtubeVideos.desc: Simple explanation of how buzzers make sound.

youtubeVideos.title: Simple Buzzer Circuit
youtubeVideos.desc: Basic buzzer activity using battery and wires.

activity.title: Hear the Buzzer!
steps: Connect buzzer to battery using wires
Students listen carefully to the buzzing sound
Students gently touch the buzzer to feel vibration
Disconnect one wire to stop the sound
Discuss where buzzers are used in daily life

quiz.explanation: A buzzer produces sound when electricity flows through it.

quiz.q: What gives power to the buzzer?
quiz.explanation: Battery provides electrical energy to the buzzer.

quiz.q: What can students feel when touching a working buzzer carefully?

quiz.explanation: Buzzers are used in alarms, bells and electronic devices.

quiz.q: What happens when the wire is removed from the circuit?
quiz.explanation: Removing the wire breaks the circuit and stops the buzzer.

quiz.q: What creates sound in a buzzer?
quiz.explanation: Sound is created by fast vibrations inside the buzzer.

quiz.q: Which sense organ helps us hear buzzer sound?
quiz.explanation: We hear sound using our ears.

quiz.q: What should students do while handling electronics?
quiz.explanation: Electronic components should always be handled carefully and safely.

quiz.q: An active buzzer starts making sound when:
quiz.explanation: Active buzzers automatically make sound when connected to electricity.

quiz.q: Which real-life device may use a buzzer?
quiz.explanation: Electronic school bells often use buzzers to create sound.
